title: campanula moravica subsp. xylorrhiza
intro: Campanula moravica subsp. xylorrhiza, also known as the Xylorrhiza variety of the Moravian Bellflower, is a plant species in the Campanulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Campanula moravica subsp. xylorrhiza include soothing sore throats, relieving congestion, and reducing fever. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to treat respiratory infections and other health issues.
